ogTitle: South Granville vs South Gundagai: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Granville and South Gundagai.
heading: South Granville vs South Gundagai — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Granville, New South Wales and South Gundagai, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Granville has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to South Gundagai (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Granville or South Gundagai safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Granville has a flood risk score of 53/100, while South Gundagai scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Granville scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. South Gundagai scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Granville or South Gundagai?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Granville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
